code: @Manual{,   title = {fiber: S7 Data Structures for Diffusion MRI Tractography},   author = {Aymeric Stamm},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://github.com/astamm/fiber}, }

title: S7 Data Structures for Diffusion MRI Tractography
text: fiber package provides two S7 classes — streamline bundle — representing diffusion MRI tractography data R, together concise set methods:

title: S7 Data Structures for Diffusion MRI Tractography
text: streamline stores: @points — n×3n \times 3 numeric matrix columns "X", "Y", "Z". @point_data — named list per-point numeric vectors (length nn). @streamline_data — named list per-streamline numeric scalars. bundle stores: @streamlines — list streamline objects. @bundle_data — named list bundle-level metadata.

title: S7 Data Structures for Diffusion MRI Tractography
text: can install development version fiber GitHub :

title: S7 Data Structures for Diffusion MRI Tractography
code: library(fiber)  # Build a helix streamline (50 points) t <- seq(0, 2 * pi, length.out = 50) sl <- new_streamline(   points = cbind(X = cos(t), Y = sin(t), Z = t / (2 * pi)) ) sl #> <streamline [50 pts]>  # Shape descriptors get_curvilinear_length(sl) #> [1] 6.358015 get_sinuosity(sl) #> [1] 6.358015 head(get_curvature(sl)) #>           s    curvature #> 1 0.0000000 1.537338e-05 #> 2 0.1297554 1.625127e-02 #> 3 0.2595108 4.592627e-02 #> 4 0.3892662 8.636675e-02 #> 5 0.5190216 1.352354e-01 #> 6 0.6487770 1.906608e-01  # Bundle two streamlines sl2 <- new_streamline(   points = cbind(X = cos(t) * 1.1, Y = sin(t) * 1.1, Z = t / (2 * pi)) ) b <- bind_bundles(sl, sl2) b #> <bundle [2 streamlines | 50–50 pts/streamline]>  # Reparametrize to 20 points each b20 <- reparametrize(b, n_points = 20L) b20 #> <bundle [2 streamlines | 20–20 pts/streamline]>  # Hausdorff distance get_hausdorff_distance(sl, sl2) #> [1] 0.1

what: Combine streamlines and/or bundles into a single bundle — bind_bundles
title: Combine streamlines and/or bundles into a single bundle — bind_bundles
text: Accepts mix streamline bundle objects.  streamlines collected flat list wrapped new bundle.  bundle_data first bundle argument () preserved; pass via bundle_data argument override.

title: Combine streamlines and/or bundles into a single bundle — bind_bundles
Combine streamlines and/or bundles into a single bundle — bind_bundles
text: ... One streamline bundle objects. bundle_data named list bundle-level metadata attach resulting bundle.  Defaults empty list (bundle_data first bundle input one present bundle_data supplied).

title: Combine streamlines and/or bundles into a single bundle — bind_bundles
text: bundle containing input streamlines.

title: The bundle S7 class — bundle
text: bundle ordered collection streamline objects representing tractogram white-matter bundle.  stores two compartments: @streamlines — list streamline objects. @bundle_data — named list bundle-level metadata (arbitrary R objects, e.g. affine transform used tracking).

title: The bundle S7 class — bundle
The bundle S7 class — bundle
text: streamlines list streamline objects. bundle_data named list bundle-level metadata.

title: The bundle S7 class — bundle
text: Use new_bundle() constructor create instances.

title: Curvature of a streamline — get_curvature
text: Computes curvature \(\kappa(s)\) point along arc-length abscissa using cubic smoothing splines (3 degrees freedom per component).

title: Curvature of a streamline — get_curvature
Curvature of a streamline — get_curvature
text: x streamline object. scalar One "mean", "sd", "max", NULL (default). NULL full profile returned data frame columns s curvature.

title: Curvature of a streamline — get_curvature
text: Either data.frame (scalar = NULL) single numeric value.

what: Curvilinear length of a streamline — get_curvilinear_length
title: Curvilinear length of a streamline — get_curvilinear_length
text: total arc-length streamline computed sum Euclidean segment lengths consecutive points.

title: Curvilinear length of a streamline — get_curvilinear_length
Curvilinear length of a streamline — get_curvilinear_length
text: x streamline object.

title: Curvilinear length of a streamline — get_curvilinear_length
text: non-negative numeric scalar.

what: Euclidean length of a streamline — get_euclidean_length
title: Euclidean length of a streamline — get_euclidean_length
text: Euclidean (straight-line) distance two endpoints.

title: Euclidean length of a streamline — get_euclidean_length
Euclidean length of a streamline — get_euclidean_length
text: x streamline object.

title: Euclidean length of a streamline — get_euclidean_length

what: Hausdorff distance between two streamlines — get_hausdorff_distance
title: Hausdorff distance between two streamlines — get_hausdorff_distance
text: Computes symmetric Hausdorff distance two streamline objects based 3-D coordinate matrices.

title: Hausdorff distance between two streamlines — get_hausdorff_distance
Hausdorff distance between two streamlines — get_hausdorff_distance
text: x streamline object. y streamline object.

title: Hausdorff distance between two streamlines — get_hausdorff_distance
text: non-negative numeric scalar.

title: Sinuosity of a streamline — get_sinuosity
text: ratio curvilinear length Euclidean length.  value 1 indicates perfectly straight streamline; larger values indicate greater curviness.

title: Sinuosity of a streamline — get_sinuosity
Sinuosity of a streamline — get_sinuosity
text: x streamline object.

title: Sinuosity of a streamline — get_sinuosity
text: numeric scalar \(\ge 1\).

title: Torsion of a streamline — get_torsion
text: Computes torsion \(\tau(s)\) point along arc-length abscissa using cubic smoothing splines (4 degrees freedom per component).

title: Torsion of a streamline — get_torsion
Torsion of a streamline — get_torsion
text: x streamline object. scalar One "mean", "sd", "max", NULL (default). NULL full profile returned data frame columns s torsion.

title: Torsion of a streamline — get_torsion
text: Either data.frame (scalar = NULL) single numeric value.

text: convenience constructor wraps bundle S7 class.

title: Create a bundle object — new_bundle
Create a bundle object — new_bundle
text: streamlines list streamline objects. bundle_data named list bundle-level metadata.  Defaults empty list.

title: Create a streamline object — new_streamline
text: convenience constructor wraps streamline S7 class.

title: Create a streamline object — new_streamline
Create a streamline object — new_streamline
text: points numeric matrix least three columns named "X", "Y", "Z". Rows correspond ordered points along tract. point_data named list numeric vectors, length nrow(points), holding per-point scalar attributes.  Defaults empty list. streamline_data named list numeric scalars (length-1 vectors) holding per-streamline attributes.  Defaults empty list.

title: Create a streamline object — new_streamline
text: object class streamline.

what: Reparametrize a streamline or bundle onto a uniform arc-length grid — reparametrize
title: Reparametrize a streamline or bundle onto a uniform arc-length grid — reparametrize
text: Resamples 3-D coordinates (@point_data attributes) streamline every streamline inside bundle onto uniform arc-length grid using linear interpolation.

title: Reparametrize a streamline or bundle onto a uniform arc-length grid — reparametrize
Reparametrize a streamline or bundle onto a uniform arc-length grid — reparametrize
text: x streamline bundle object. n_points Number equally-spaced arc-length points use. single streamline, defaults nrow(x@points). bundle, defaults rounded mean number points across streamlines. Pass NULL use defaults explicitly.

title: Reparametrize a streamline or bundle onto a uniform arc-length grid — reparametrize
text: object class x reparametrized onto new grid.

title: The streamline S7 class — streamline
text: streamline represents single fibre tract. stores three data compartments mirror conceptual levels found tractography file formats: @points — \(n \times 3\) numeric matrix whose columns named "X", "Y", "Z", holding ordered 3-D coordinates \(n\) points along tract. @point_data — named list numeric vectors, length \(n\), holding per-point scalar attributes (e.g. fractional anisotropy sampled every point). @streamline_data — named list numeric scalars (length-1 vectors) holding per-streamline attributes (e.g. tract-level weight mean FA).

title: The streamline S7 class — streamline
code: streamline(points = NULL, point_data = list(), streamline_data = list())

title: The streamline S7 class — streamline
text: points numeric matrix columns "X", "Y", "Z". point_data named list per-point numeric vectors. streamline_data named list per-streamline numeric scalars.

title: The streamline S7 class — streamline
text: Use new_streamline() constructor create instances. Slots accessed @ operator: sl@points, sl@point_data, sl@streamline_data.

title: fiber 0.1.0
text: package rebuilt scratch around two S7 classes: streamline bundle. previous tibble-based streamline list-based tract removed.

title: fiber 0.1.0
text: new_streamline() / new_bundle() constructors S7 classes. bind_bundles() combines mix streamlines bundles single bundle. reparametrize() resamples streamline every streamline bundle onto uniform arc-length grid. get_euclidean_length(), get_curvilinear_length(), get_sinuosity() — geometric shape scalars. get_curvature(), get_torsion() — full curvature/torsion profiles summary scalars ("mean", "sd", "max"). get_hausdorff_distance() — symmetric Hausdorff distance two streamlines.

title: fiber 0.1.0
text: Runtime dependencies reduced S7, rlang, cli .
